data.title: Mastercard (NYSE: MA) beats Q4 expectations with $8.81B revenues, up 18% Y/Y. Adjusted EPS at $4.76, up 25% Y/Y. Q1 revenue growth expected in low teens. Fiscal 2026 revenue growth expected at high end of low double digits. Share updates on Benzinga.com.
data.description: In the latest financial report, Mastercard Inc. (NYSE: MA) announced a strong performance, surpassing expectations. The company's fourth-quarter net revenues stood at $8.81 billion, marking an 18% increase year-over-year and a 15% rise on a currency-neutral basis. This exceeded the analyst consensus of $8.79 billion. The adjusted earnings per share (EPS) also saw a significant uptick, climbing 25% year-over-year to reach $4.76, well above the forecast of $4.25. Looking ahead, Mastercard foresees net revenue growth in the low teens for the first quarter, in contrast to the analyst consensus of $8.3 billion. In terms of fiscal 2026, the company anticipates revenue growth at the high end of low double-digits, outperforming the analyst consensus of $36.8 billion. Market reaction to Mastercard's report has been positive. For more details, the full news article is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Research report on Smart Metering in Latin America and the Caribbean includes profiles of 18 meter vendors, 5 communications solution providers, 6 software solution providers, and 10 system integrators. Over 200 pages of industry forecasts and case studies.
data.description: A new report titled "Smart Metering in Latin America and the Caribbean - 3rd Edition" has been recently included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's available reports. This comprehensive report offers valuable insights through over 200 pages of exclusive business intelligence. It includes 6-year industry forecasts, expert insights, and real-life case studies to assist in making informed business decisions. Smart metering is seen as essential for the development of future smart grids and is undergoing widespread deployment in the region.

data.title: Latin American fintech Nubank secures conditional approval to establish a national bank in the US, allowing it to offer regulated banking and crypto services.
data.description: Conditional approval has been granted to a Latin American fintech company, allowing it to progress into the bank organization phase as it aims to broaden its range of regulated banking and crypto services in the United States.

data.title: Hubbell Inc. declares $1.42/share regular quarterly dividend, payable on March 16, 2026 to shareholders of record on Feb 27.
data.description: On January 30, 2026, the Board of Directors at Hubbell Incorporated (NYSE: HUBB) announced a quarterly dividend of $1.42 per share for its common stock. Shareholders as of the record date on February 27, 2026, will receive the dividend on March 16, 2026.

data.title: VGP to review FY 2025 financial results via webcast on Feb 19, 2026. Company provides logistics/semi-industrial real estate.
data.description: On January 30, 2026, in Antwerp, Belgium, VGP NV, a leading European provider of top-notch logistics and semi-industrial real estate, unveiled information about an upcoming webcast to discuss its financial performance for the year concluding on December 31, 2025.

data.title: Analysts raise forecasts for Caterpillar after strong Q4 results. Q4 sales $19.133B, up 18%. Adjusted profit/share $5.16.CEO positive for future.
data.description: Caterpillar Inc. (NYSE: CAT) released its fourth-quarter earnings report, showcasing strong results. Sales and revenues for Q4 2025 totaled $19.133 billion, marking an 18% increase from $16.215 billion in the same period last year. The adjusted profit per share stood at $5.16, surpassing the $4.66 estimate, with revenue also exceeding expectations at $17.851 billion. In terms of GAAP figures, the fourth-quarter profit per diluted share was $5.12, down from $5.78 in the previous year. Operating profit saw a 9% decline to $2.660 billion, resulting in an operating profit margin of 13.9%, a decrease from 18.0% in Q4 2024. Caterpillar's CEO Joe Creed expressed optimism about the company's performance, highlighting a record backlog and emphasizing their commitment to providing long-term value to customers and shareholders. Despite the positive results, Caterpillar's shares experienced a decline. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Blockchain Commodity Storage market valued at $1.27 billion in 2024, poised for rapid growth - projected to reach $1.63 billion in 2025.
data.description: Dublin - A new report titled "Blockchain Commodity Storage Platform Global Market Report 2025" is now available on ResearchAndMarkets.com. The market for blockchain commodity storage platforms has seen rapid growth and is projected to continue expanding. In 2024, the market was valued at $1.27 billion, with expectations to reach $1.63 billion in 2025, showcasing a notable Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 28.4%. This growth is a clear indication of the rising popularity of blockchain technology, driven by the need for secure commodity storage solutions.

data.title: Garrett Motion renews partnership with Scuderia Ferrari HP to supply turbo system for 2026 FIA regulation cycle.
data.description: Garrett Motion Inc. (Nasdaq: GTX), based in Plymouth, Mich., and Switzerland's Rolle, revealed on Jan. 30, 2026, that they have renewed their enduring technical collaboration with Scuderia Ferrari HP. The partnership entails Garrett engineering and delivering the advanced turbo system for Ferrari's Formula 1 power unit for the upcoming 2026 FIA regulations. Garrett, renowned for its expertise in high-performance turbocharging and electrification technologies, solidifies its connection with the racing world by continuing this critical alliance with Ferrari.

data.title: Souper Bowl of Caring campaign in San Francisco raises $220 million for food charities, supporting families in need.
data.description: In San Francisco, as the Big Game comes to town, attention is being drawn to Bay Area families experiencing food insecurity. The 36th annual Souper Bowl of Caring is shining a light on this issue and hosting various local events to provide support. Initiated in 1990, Tackle Hunger's Souper Bowl of Caring has raised over $220 million for food charities across the country. This year's efforts aim to ensure that no community member goes without a nourishing bowl of soup, emphasizing the importance of community-led initiatives in addressing hunger.

data.title: Conclusion of share buyback programme involving multiple stock exchanges, Danish FSA, and other stakeholders.
data.description: Various stakeholders including Nasdaq Copenhagen, Euronext Dublin, and the London Stock Exchange, as well as the Danish Financial Supervisory Authority, have come together to address issues in the financial industry. This collaboration aims to improve transparency and compliance within the sector. These initiatives involve a wide range of participants, with a focus on promoting market stability and investor confidence.

data.title: SKF has sold its precision elastomeric device (PED) operation in Elgin, USA as part of strategic focus on aerospace.
data.description: In Gothenburg, Sweden, on January 30, 2026, SKF finalized the divestment of its precision elastomeric device (PED) operation located in Elgin, Illinois, USA. This move is in alignment with SKF's strategic plan to concentrate on its core aerospace sectors and discontinue non-core business ventures.

data.title: Honeywell Q4 earnings beat with $2.59/share, $9.76B revenue; F25 forecast: $10.35–$10.65 EPS, $38.8–$39.8B sales. Q1 outlook: $2.25–$2.35 EPS, $9.1–$9.4B revenue. CEO mentions $37B backlog.
data.description: Honeywell International Inc. (NASDAQ: HON) released its fourth-quarter 2025 results and guidance, with adjusted earnings reaching $2.59 per share, surpassing the anticipated $2.54. Quarterly revenue increased by 6% YoY to $9.76 billion, slightly below the projected $9.85 billion. Honeywell forecasts adjusted EPS for fiscal 2026 to range between $10.35 and $10.65, compared to analyst predictions of $10.38, and expects sales to reach $38.8 billion-$39.8 billion, falling short of the estimated $39.62 billion. For Q1, adjusted EPS is projected to be $2.25–$2.35, below the consensus of $2.34, with revenue expected in the range of $9.1 billion-$9.4 billion, as opposed to the projected $9.29 billion. Vimal Kapur, chairman and CEO of Honeywell, emphasized the company's record backlog exceeding $37 billion, positioning them for success.

data.title: Shareholders approve financial statements of Ascencio SA for the year ended Sept 2025 at General Meeting on Jan 30, 2026.
data.description: On January 30, 2026, the Ordinary General Meeting of Shareholders gave approval to all resolutions proposed on the agenda, primarily focusing on the financial statements of Ascencio SA for the fiscal year ending on September 30, 2025.

data.title: Abrigo acquires Journey Technology Solutions for data analytics innovation in financial institutions.
data.description: In Austin, Texas, Abrigo has recently completed the acquisition of Journey Technology Solutions. This move is aimed at boosting data analytics capabilities for financial institutions. The acquisition is expected to pave the way for greater innovation in this sector.
